description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, test, review, staging, production, cleanup using the alpine image for a simple build job and the ayufan/openshift-cli image for OpenShift deployment jobs. In the build stage add a build job that runs `echo build` on alpine. In the test stage add test1 and test2 jobs that each run `echo run tests`. In the review stage add a review job that runs the OpenShift login (`oc login "$OPENSHIFT_SERVER" --token="$OPENSHIFT_TOKEN" --insecure-skip-tls-verify`), ensures the project exists (`oc project "$CI_PROJECT_NAME" || oc new-project "$CI_PROJECT_NAME"`), creates the app if missing (`oc get services $APP || oc new-app . --name=$APP --strategy=docker`), exposes a route if missing (`oc get routes $APP || oc expose service $APP --hostname=$APP_HOST`), and starts a build (`oc start-build $APP --from-dir=. --wait`); it uses the ayufan/openshift-cli image, runs on all branches except master, defines environment review/$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME with on_stop stop-review and URL http://$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME.review.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN, and sets variables APP=review-$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME and APP_HOST=$CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME.review.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN. Add staging and production jobs that use the same OpenShift script, run only on the master branch, are manual, set environment names staging and production with URLs http://$CI_PROJECT_NAME-staging.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN and http://$CI_PROJECT_NAME.$OPENSHIFT_DOMAIN respectively, and define APP and APP_HOST variables accordingly. Also add a stop-review cleanup job that runs `oc delete all -l "app=$APP"` on manual trigger, stops the review environment, and sets GIT_STRATEGY=none.
